cd /home/janfr/dev/pc-166
gh pr create --draft --title "folder_sync: include-override filter rules via native rsync filter syntax (#166)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fixes #166

## Summary

Replaces `folder_sync`'s exclude-only config surface with **native rsync filter syntax** (`+`/`-`, first-match-wins), so a user can express "exclude a folder but keep select subfolders" — e.g. drop most of `~/.cache` but keep `~/.cache/uv` and `~/.cache/pip`. Two authoring surfaces:

- **Central filter file (per folder)** — a new optional `filter_file` field on each folder entry (replaces `excludes`), fed to rsync as `--filter='merge <file>'`.
- **Per-directory `.pcswitcher-filter`** — a single tree-wide `--filter='dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter'` rule; behaves like `.gitignore` (inherits into subdirs) and the files themselves sync to the target (no `e` modifier).

**Precedence: GLOBAL-FIRST.** `_build_rsync_cmd` emits filter args in order: runtime-protection excludes (ADR-016, un-overridable) → central `merge` (when set) → `dir-merge` (always). A per-dir `+` can never re-expose a globally-excluded path on a `--delete` mirror.

Other changes:
- `pc-switcher init` now ships starter `home.filter` + `root.filter` (package data) next to `config.yaml`, honoring `--force`.
- `validate()` fails fast with a source-side `ValidationError` when a configured `filter_file` is absent (after `~`/env expansion), guarding against a silent full-mirror.
- Never passes `-F`/`-FF`/`-C`/`--cvs-exclude`, so a stray/hostile `.rsync-filter` in the tree has zero effect.
- ADR-016 and `docs/configuration.md` updated: the new filter surface, precedence, a "coming from .gitignore" guide, and the ancestor-descent idiom.
- Shipped filter files use **floating** (non-leading-slash) patterns because `/home` syncs with user dirs one level below the transfer root — a leading-slash `/.cache/...` would anchor to `/home` and silently fail to match.

## Test plan

- `uv run pytest` — 594 passed (unit + real-rsync acceptance). The 3 issue #166 acceptance criteria run against the **real rsync binary** via `--dry-run` in `tests/local_rsync/` (new `local_rsync` marker, runs in the default gate):
  - central filter keeps `.cache/uv`/`.cache/pip` while dropping the rest of `.cache` at a `<root>/<user>/.cache` tree;
  - a `.pcswitcher-filter` in a subdir takes effect, inherits into deeper dirs, and itself transfers;
  - a planted hostile `.rsync-filter` is inert;
  - plus global-first enforcement (central `-` cannot be re-exposed by a per-dir `+`).
- `uv run basedpyright` — 0 errors. `uv run ruff check/format` — clean. `uv run codespell` — clean.
- VM e2e config migrated `excludes` → `filter_file`; full behavioral pass runs on integration CI (draft PR keeps it from firing until ready).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
The work is complete and the PR is up. I'm monitoring integration CI in the background and will report when it concludes. Summary of where things stand:

## Issue #166 → PR #173 (ready for review)

**What shipped** (branch `166-sync-filters`, 8 commits): `folder_sync` now uses native rsync filter syntax with two authoring surfaces, replacing the exclude-only `excludes` list.

- **Per-folder `filter_file`** → `--filter='merge <file>'`; **tree-wide `.pcswitcher-filter`** → `--filter='dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter'` (inherits like `.gitignore`, syncs to target).
- **GLOBAL-FIRST** precedence: runtime-protection excludes (ADR-016) → central `merge` → `dir-merge`. A per-dir `+` can never re-expose a globally-excluded path on a `--delete` mirror — the precedence decision you confirmed.
- `init` ships starter `home.filter`/`root.filter`; `validate()` fails fast on a missing configured filter file; never passes `-F`/`-FF`/`-C`.
- ADR-016 + `docs/configuration.md` updated (filter surface, precedence, coming-from-`.gitignore` guide, ancestor-descent idiom).

**Verification** (independently re-run, not just trusted from the executor):
- 594 unit tests pass; the **3 issue #166 acceptance criteria run against the real rsync binary** (`tests/local_rsync/`) plus a global-first enforcement test — all green.
- `basedpyright` 0 errors, ruff + codespell clean; no `excludes` config field remains.
- GSD verifier: **passed** (6/6 must-haves). Code review: **no blockers** — I fixed the one WARNING (a docs self-contradiction) and one INFO (init error boundary) in commit `2a2c003`.

**CI:** Lint, Unit Tests, CI Status all pass. Integration VM suite now running (I marked the PR ready to exercise the migrated e2e config, per your practice of running integration CI proactively).

One design refinement worth flagging: the planner correctly caught that shipped filter files must use **floating** (non-leading-slash) patterns — `/home` syncs with user homes one level below the transfer root, so a leading-slash `/.cache/...` would anchor to `/home` and silently break the `.cache/uv` keep. This corrected my initial CONTEXT idiom, and there's a real-rsync test proving it at a `<root>/<user>/.cache` tree.

I'll report back when integration CI finishes; if it fails I'll fix, re-verify, and push.

1	"""Real-rsync acceptance tests for folder_sync filter rules (issue #166).
2	
3	These tests need only a local `rsync` binary — no VM, no SSH. Each test shells
4	out to the real rsync via subprocess against a source/destination tree built
5	under `tmp_path`, using `-a --delete --dry-run --out-format=%n` plus the
6	filter args under test, and asserts on the set of transferred relative paths.
7	Nothing is ever written (--dry-run); tests skip cleanly when rsync is absent.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import shutil
13	import subprocess
14	from importlib.resources import files
15	from pathlib import Path
16	
17	import pytest
18	
19	pytestmark = [
20	    pytest.mark.local_rsync,
21	    pytest.mark.skipif(shutil.which("rsync") is None, reason="requires local rsync binary"),
22	]
23	
24	
25	def run_rsync(src: Path, dst: Path, *filter_args: str) -> set[str]:
26	    """Run real rsync in --dry-run mode; return the set of transferred relative paths.
27	
28	    Trailing slashes (rsync prints them for directory entries) are stripped so
29	    a directory and a file of the same name compare equal — not a concern for
30	    these fixtures, but keeps the returned set predictable.
31	    """
32	    cmd = [
33	        "rsync",
34	        "-a",
35	        "--delete",
36	        "--dry-run",
37	        "--out-format=%n",
38	        *filter_args,
39	        f"{src}/",
40	        f"{dst}/",
41	    ]
42	    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
43	    return {line.rstrip("/") for line in result.stdout.splitlines() if line.strip()}
44	
45	
46	def _write(path: Path, content: str = "content") -> None:
47	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
48	    path.write_text(content)
49	
50	
51	class TestCentralMergeCacheIncludeOverride:
52	    """Acceptance #1: central `merge` filter at a /home-style transfer root.
53	
54	    Proves floating (non-leading-slash) patterns keep .cache/uv and .cache/pip
55	    while dropping the rest of .cache, at a transfer root where each user's
56	    directory sits one level below (alice/.cache/uv, not /.cache/uv) — the
57	    FLAGGED PLANNER DECISION in the plan's CONTEXT.
58	    """
59	
60	    def test_shipped_home_filter_keeps_dev_caches_drops_rest(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
61	        src = tmp_path / "src"
62	        dst = tmp_path / "dst"
63	        _write(src / "alice/.cache/uv/pkg1")

Yes. Three tiers, two of which use no mocks at all:

**1. Real-rsync behavioral tests** — `tests/local_rsync/test_folder_sync_filters.py` (shown above). These shell out to the actual `rsync` binary via `subprocess` against real files under `tmp_path` and assert on what rsync actually transfers (`--dry-run --out-format=%n`). No mocks. They prove the four behaviors that matter: the `.cache/uv` keep, `dir-merge` inheritance + self-transfer, hostile `.rsync-filter` inertness, and global-first enforcement. They run in the default `uv run pytest` gate (skip only if no `rsync` binary).

**2. Full VM end-to-end** — let me confirm what it actually exercises.

content.text:
Here's the honest, layered picture — there are two non-mock tiers, plus one coverage seam worth naming:

## Real (non-mock) coverage

**Real rsync binary** — `tests/local_rsync/` (4 tests, in the default gate). Shell out to actual `rsync`, assert on what it really transfers. These are the *only* tests that exercise the genuinely new behaviors — the `+` include-override (`.cache/uv` keep), `dir-merge` inheritance + self-transfer, hostile `.rsync-filter` inertness, and global-first. No transport is faked; only SSH is absent (local src→dst).

**Real VMs, real SSH, real everything** — `test_end_to_end_sync.py`. Runs `pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes` machine-to-machine through the actual `_build_rsync_cmd` → rsync-over-SSH pipeline with a `filter_file` seeded on the source VM, then compares source/target md5 manifests. This is what ran 15m35s green on #173.

## The seam (stated plainly, not hidden)

- The `local_rsync` tests hand-build the rsync argv in their `run_rsync` helper — they prove *rsync's* behavior, not that `_build_rsync_cmd` emits those exact args. That mapping is covered by the **mocked** unit tests (`test_folder_sync.py`, which assert the command string).
- The VM e2e closes the full-stack loop (real command builder + real rsync + real SSH), but its filter file uses **only `-` exclude rules** (`_make_e2e_home_filter`, lines 286-296). So the two *new* mechanisms — `+` re-includes and per-dir `.pcswitcher-filter` — are **not** exercised end-to-end on real machines. They're proven by the real-binary tests (no SSH) and the mocked argv tests, which meet in the middle by construction rather than in one end-to-end test.

If you'd want that seam closed, the cheap high-value add is extending the VM e2e to seed a `.cache/uv` keep rule and a per-dir `.pcswitcher-filter`, then assert `.cache/uv` and the inherited exclusion actually land correctly on the target — turning the "meet in the middle" into a single real-machine proof of the new paths. Want me to add that to the PR?

Indeed, change the VM e2e config.yaml and fileset-that-gets-synced so we touch and verify all cases:
- global filter file
- per-dir-filter file, nested
- includes and excludes and a misc (similar to the example from #166)
Verifying that
- what should be included effectively deletes/overwrites/adds files on target
- that what should be excluded effectively leaves the target as-is, in case it (or something conflicting) exists on the source and in case nothing of that exists on the source at all (important difference!)
- per-dir-filter files are transfered
Let me read the full e2e test to understand its structure — the test tree, seeding, and verification pattern — before changing it.

300	    """Config exercising both a generic job (dummy_success) and folder_sync of /home."""
301	    return """\
302	logging:
303	  file: DEBUG
304	  tui: INFO
305	  external: WARNING
306	sync_jobs:
307	  dummy_success: true
308	  folder_sync: true
309	disk_space_monitor:
310	  preflight_minimum: "5%"
311	  runtime_minimum: "3%"
312	  warning_threshold: "10%"
313	  check_interval: 5
314	btrfs_snapshots:
315	  subvolumes:
316	    - "@"
317	    - "@home"
318	  keep_recent: 2
319	dummy_success:
320	  source_duration: 2
321	  target_duration: 2
322	folder_sync:
323	  folders:
324	    - path: /home
325	      enabled: true
326	      filter_file: ~/.config/pc-switcher/home.filter
327	"""

330	async def _write_config(executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor, config: str) -> None:
331	    """Write the pc-switcher config to a VM."""
332	    result = await executor.run_command(
333	        f"mkdir -p ~/.config/pc-switcher && cat > ~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml << 'CONF_EOF'\n{config}CONF_EOF",
334	        timeout=10.0,
335	    )
336	    assert result.success, f"Failed to write config: {result.stderr}"

339	async def _write_filter_file(executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor, contents: str) -> None:
340	    """Write the folder_sync filter_file referenced by the e2e config to a VM (#166)."""
341	    cmd = (
342	        "mkdir -p ~/.config/pc-switcher && "
343	        f"cat > ~/.config/pc-switcher/home.filter << 'FILTER_EOF'\n{contents}FILTER_EOF"
344	    )
345	    result = await executor.run_command(cmd, timeout=10.0)
346	    assert result.success, f"Failed to write filter file: {result.stderr}"

349	async def _seed_rich_tree(executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor, tree: str) -> None:
350	    """Create the rich metadata/ownership test tree inside `tree` on a VM.
351	
352	    Covers the full ownership x permission matrix (user/root/other-user files AND
353	    directories), special permission bits (setuid/setgid/sticky), a POSIX ACL, a
354	    backdated mtime, a hard-link pair, a relative symlink, and a config-excluded
355	    subtree. Root-/other-user-owned entries are created then chowned, so rsync-as-root
356	    must read and preserve entries the invoking user cannot access.
357	    """
358	    result = await executor.run_command(
359	        f"""set -e
360	T={tree}
361	rm -rf "$T"
362	mkdir -p "$T"/d700 "$T"/d755 "$T"/setgid_dir "$T"/sticky_dir "$T"/secret
363	
364	# User-owned files with varied permission bits
365	printf 'content-600' > "$T/f600.txt"; chmod 600 "$T/f600.txt"
366	printf 'content-640' > "$T/f640.txt"; chmod 640 "$T/f640.txt"
367	printf 'content-644' > "$T/f644.txt"; chmod 644 "$T/f644.txt"
368	printf 'content-755' > "$T/f755.txt"; chmod 755 "$T/f755.txt"
369	printf 'content-777' > "$T/f777.txt"; chmod 777 "$T/f777.txt"
370	printf 'content-suid' > "$T/setuid.bin"; chmod 4755 "$T/setuid.bin"
371	printf 'content-sgid' > "$T/setgid.bin"; chmod 2755 "$T/setgid.bin"
372	
373	# User-owned directories with varied permission bits (each non-empty)
374	printf 'in-d700'   > "$T/d700/inside.txt";       chmod 700  "$T/d700"
375	printf 'in-d755'   > "$T/d755/inside.txt";       chmod 755  "$T/d755"
376	printf 'in-sgid'   > "$T/setgid_dir/inside.txt"; chmod 2775 "$T/setgid_dir"
377	printf 'in-sticky' > "$T/sticky_dir/inside.txt"; chmod 1777 "$T/sticky_dir"
378	
379	# POSIX ACL (numeric uid, need not exist on either machine)
380	printf 'content-acl' > "$T/acl.txt"; setfacl -m u:2001:r "$T/acl.txt"
381	
382	# Backdated mtime
383	printf 'content-backdated' > "$T/backdated.txt"
384	touch -d "@{_BACKDATED_MTIME}" "$T/backdated.txt"
385	
386	# Hard-link pair and relative symlink
387	printf 'content-hardlink' > "$T/hl_a.txt"
388	ln "$T/hl_a.txt" "$T/hl_b.txt"
389	ln -s f644.txt "$T/sym.txt"
390	
391	# Root-owned file and directory (created as the user, then chowned; the user
392	# ends up with no access, and rsync-as-root must still read and preserve them).
393	printf 'content-root-file' > "$T/root_file.txt"
394	sudo chown 0:0 "$T/root_file.txt"; sudo chmod 600 "$T/root_file.txt"
395	mkdir -p "$T/root_dir"; printf 'content-root-dir' > "$T/root_dir/inside.txt"
396	sudo chown -R 0:0 "$T/root_dir"
397	sudo chmod 700 "$T/root_dir"; sudo chmod 600 "$T/root_dir/inside.txt"
398	
399	# Other-(system-)user-owned file and directory (invoking user has no access)
400	printf 'content-other-file' > "$T/other_file.txt"
401	sudo chown {_OTHER_UID}:{_OTHER_GID} "$T/other_file.txt"; sudo chmod 600 "$T/other_file.txt"
402	mkdir -p "$T/other_dir"; printf 'content-other-dir' > "$T/other_dir/inside.txt"
403	sudo chown -R {_OTHER_UID}:{_OTHER_GID} "$T/other_dir"
404	sudo chmod 700 "$T/other_dir"; sudo chmod 600 "$T/other_dir/inside.txt"
405	
406	# Excluded subtree (must never reach the target)
407	printf 'top-secret' > "$T/secret/token.txt"
408	""",
409	        timeout=60.0,
410	        login_shell=False,
411	    )
412	    assert result.success, f"Failed to seed rich test tree: {result.stderr}"
413	
414	
415	def _manifest_cmd(tree: str) -> str:
416	    """Command emitting a deterministic `<type> <mode> <uid> <gid> <path>` manifest of `tree`.
417	
418	    Runs under sudo (root-/other-user-owned entries readable); the excluded
419	    `secret/` subtree is pruned so source and target manifests match on success.
420	    """
421	    return (
422	        f"cd {tree} && sudo find . -path ./secret -prune -o "
423	        r"\( -type f -o -type d -o -type l \) -printf '%y %m %U %G %p\n' | LC_ALL=C sort"
424	    )
425	
426	
427	def _md5_manifest_cmd(tree: str) -> str:
428	    """Command emitting C-sorted md5sums of every regular file in `tree` (symlinks/secret skipped)."""
429	    return (
430	        f"cd {tree} && sudo find . -path ./secret -prune -o -type f ! -type l "
431	        r"-exec md5sum {} + | LC_ALL=C sort"
432	    )
433	
434	
435	async def _seed_included_markers(executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor) -> None:
436	    """Seed the SC3 inclusion/exclusion marker files in the real home dotdirs."""
437	    parts = ["set -e"]
438	    for rel, content in _INCLUDED_MARKERS.items():
439	        parts.append(f'mkdir -p ~/"$(dirname {rel})" && printf %s {content!r} > ~/{rel}')
440	    parts.append(f'mkdir -p ~/"$(dirname {_EXCLUDED_MARKER})" && printf excluded > ~/{_EXCLUDED_MARKER}')
441	    result = await executor.run_command("\n".join(parts), timeout=15.0, login_shell=False)
442	    assert result.success, f"Failed to seed inclusion markers: {result.stderr}"
443	
444	
445	async def _remove_test_artifacts(
446	    pc1_exec: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
447	    pc2_exec: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
448	    tree: str,
449	) -> None:
450	    """Remove the seeded test subtree, inclusion markers, and config from both VMs."""
451	    markers = " ".join(f"~/{rel}" for rel in (*_INCLUDED_MARKERS, _EXCLUDED_MARKER))
452	    for name, exec_ in (("pc1", pc1_exec), ("pc2", pc2_exec)):
453	        res = await exec_.run_command(
454	            f"sudo rm -rf {tree} {markers} && rm -f ~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml",
455	            timeout=30.0,
456	            login_shell=False,
457	        )
458	        if not res.success:
459	            print(f"[cleanup] {name} removal warning: {res.stderr}")

462	class TestEndToEndSync:
463	    """Integration tests for complete pc-switcher sync workflow."""
464	
465	    async def test_core_us_job_arch_as1_job_integration_via_interface(  # noqa: PLR0915
466	        self,
467	        pc1_with_pcswitcher_mod: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
468	        pc2_with_pcswitcher: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
469	        reset_pcswitcher_state: None,
470	        pc1_executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
471	        pc2_executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
472	    ) -> None:
473	        """CORE-US-JOB-ARCH-AS1 + full folder-sync end-to-end (criteria 1-5, ADR-014/015/016).
474	
475	        One scenario, one seed, five syncs — the complete pipeline in a single run:
476	
477	        1. Blocked A→B (no flag): the W1 first-sync gate aborts non-interactively; nothing reaches pc2.
478	        2. A→B --dry-run: rehearses through the gate (ADR-014) but writes nothing and does not update history (D-12).
479	        3. A→B --allow-first-sync: the real sync. Verifies BOTH
480	           - job integration via the standardized interface (dummy_success + folder_sync discovered,
481	             logged, pre/post snapshots on both machines, config synced), AND
482	           - folder_sync of the real /home: byte-identical content; numeric uid/gid; permissions incl.
483	             setuid/setgid/sticky; POSIX ACL; mtime; hard-link inode sharing; symlink; across user-,
484	             root-, and other-user-owned files AND directories the invoking user cannot read; config
485	             exclusions honoured; and the ADR-016 runtime-file excludes (state/install/logs) via sentinels.
486	        4. Mutate pc2 (add / modify / delete file / delete directory / chmod) then B→A: all propagate.
487	        5. A→B again with no override: a clean round-trip must not trip the out-of-order gate (ADR-015 #159).
488	
489	        See the module-level "Folder-sync scenario helpers" for why syncing the real /home is safe here.
490	        """
491	        _ = (pc1_with_pcswitcher_mod, pc2_with_pcswitcher, reset_pcswitcher_state)
492	        user = os.environ["PC_SWITCHER_TEST_USER"]
493	        tree = _tree(user)
494	
495	        try:
496	            await _write_config(pc1_executor, _make_e2e_config())
497	            await _write_filter_file(pc1_executor, _make_e2e_home_filter())
498	            await _seed_rich_tree(pc1_executor, tree)
499	            await _seed_included_markers(pc1_executor)
500	            await pc2_executor.run_command(f"sudo rm -rf {tree}", timeout=15.0, login_shell=False)
501	
502	            # ADR-016 runtime-exclude sentinels: a marker inside each machine's own state dir
503	            # (reset_pcswitcher_state wiped the dir, so create it fresh here).
504	            await pc1_executor.run_command(
505	                f"mkdir -p {_STATE_DIR} && printf pc1 > {_STATE_DIR}/SENTINEL_SOURCE", timeout=10.0
506	            )
507	            await pc2_executor.run_command(
508	                f"mkdir -p {_STATE_DIR} && printf pc2 > {_STATE_DIR}/SENTINEL_TARGET", timeout=10.0
509	            )
510	
511	            src_manifest = await pc1_executor.run_command(_manifest_cmd(tree), timeout=30.0, login_shell=False)
512	            assert src_manifest.success, f"source manifest failed: {src_manifest.stderr}"
513	            src_md5 = await pc1_executor.run_command(_md5_manifest_cmd(tree), timeout=30.0, login_shell=False)
514	            assert src_md5.success, f"source md5 manifest failed: {src_md5.stderr}"
515	
516	            # --- Step 1: blocked first sync (W1 gate, non-interactive) ---
517	            blocked = await pc1_executor.run_command("pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes", timeout=180.0, login_shell=True)
518	            assert not blocked.success, (
519	                f"W1 first-sync gate should block non-interactively, got exit {blocked.exit_code}.\n{blocked.stdout}"
520	            )
521	            assert (
522	                "out-of-order" in (blocked.stdout + blocked.stderr).lower()
523	                or "target" in (blocked.stdout + blocked.stderr).lower()
524	            ), f"Unexpected first-sync-gate message.\nstdout: {blocked.stdout}\nstderr: {blocked.stderr}"
525	            no_tree = await pc2_executor.run_command(f"test ! -e {tree}", timeout=10.0, login_shell=False)
526	            assert no_tree.success, "Blocked first sync transferred the tree to pc2."
527	
528	            # --- Step 2: dry-run rehearsal (proceeds, writes nothing, no history change) ---
529	            hist_before = await pc1_executor.run_command(
530	                f"cat {_STATE_DIR}/sync-history.json 2>/dev/null || echo absent", timeout=10.0
531	            )
532	            dry = await pc1_executor.run_command(
533	                "pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes --dry-run", timeout=180.0, login_shell=True
534	            )
535	            assert dry.success, f"--dry-run should not be blocked (ADR-014).\nstderr: {dry.stderr}"
536	            still_no_tree = await pc2_executor.run_command(f"test ! -e {tree}", timeout=10.0, login_shell=False)
537	            assert still_no_tree.success, "--dry-run transferred the tree to pc2 (must be read-only)."
538	            hist_after = await pc1_executor.run_command(
539	                f"cat {_STATE_DIR}/sync-history.json 2>/dev/null || echo absent", timeout=10.0
540	            )
541	            assert hist_before.stdout.strip() == hist_after.stdout.strip(), "--dry-run updated sync-history (D-12)."
542	
543	            # --- Step 3: real first sync (--allow-first-sync) ---
544	            sync_ab = await pc1_executor.run_command(
545	                "pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes --allow-first-sync", timeout=300.0, login_shell=True
546	            )
547	            assert sync_ab.success, (
548	                f"A→B first sync failed.\nexit={sync_ab.exit_code}\nstdout: {sync_ab.stdout}\nstderr: {sync_ab.stderr}"
549	            )
550	
551	            # 3a. Job integration via interface: log entries, snapshots on both, config synced.
552	            log_content = await pc1_executor.run_command(
553	                "cat $(ls -t ~/.local/share/pc-switcher/logs/sync-*.log | head -1)", timeout=10.0
554	            )
555	            assert log_content.success, f"Failed to read log file: {log_content.stderr}"
556	            log_text = log_content.stdout.lower()
557	            assert "dummy_success" in log_text or "source phase" in log_text, "Generic job (dummy_success) not logged."
558	            assert "folder_sync" in log_text, "folder_sync job not logged."
559	            src_snaps = await pc1_executor.run_command(
560	                "sudo ls /.snapshots/pc-switcher/ 2>/dev/null | head -1", timeout=10.0, login_shell=False
561	            )
562	            assert src_snaps.stdout.strip(), "Pre/post-sync snapshots missing on source."
563	            tgt_snaps = await pc2_executor.run_command(
564	                "sudo ls /.snapshots/pc-switcher/ 2>/dev/null | head -1", timeout=10.0, login_shell=False
565	            )
566	            assert tgt_snaps.stdout.strip(), "Pre/post-sync snapshots missing on target."
567	            tgt_config = await pc2_executor.run_command("cat ~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml", timeout=10.0)
568	            assert tgt_config.success and "dummy_success: true" in tgt_config.stdout, "Config not synced to target."
569	
570	            # 3b. folder_sync content + metadata: target manifests must equal source manifests exactly.
571	            tgt_manifest = await pc2_executor.run_command(_manifest_cmd(tree), timeout=30.0, login_shell=False)
572	            assert tgt_manifest.success, f"target manifest failed: {tgt_manifest.stderr}"
573	            assert tgt_manifest.stdout == src_manifest.stdout, (
574	                "Ownership/permission manifest differs after A→B (numeric uid/gid, mode, or special bits).\n"
575	                f"--- pc1 ---\n{src_manifest.stdout}\n--- pc2 ---\n{tgt_manifest.stdout}"
576	            )
577	            tgt_md5 = await pc2_executor.run_command(_md5_manifest_cmd(tree), timeout=30.0, login_shell=False)
578	            assert tgt_md5.success, f"target md5 manifest failed: {tgt_md5.stderr}"
579	            assert tgt_md5.stdout == src_md5.stdout, (
580	                "Content md5 manifest differs after A→B.\n"
581	                f"--- pc1 ---\n{src_md5.stdout}\n--- pc2 ---\n{tgt_md5.stdout}"
582	            )
583	
584	            # 3c. ACL, backdated mtime, hard-link inode sharing, symlink target.
585	            details = await pc2_executor.run_command(
586	                f"getfacl -p {tree}/acl.txt && echo '---' && "
587	                f"stat -c '%Y' {tree}/backdated.txt && "
588	                f"stat -c '%i' {tree}/hl_a.txt && stat -c '%i' {tree}/hl_b.txt && "
589	                f"readlink {tree}/sym.txt",
590	                timeout=15.0,
591	                login_shell=False,
592	            )
593	            assert details.success, f"metadata detail checks failed on pc2: {details.stderr}"
594	            acl_part, rest = details.stdout.split("---\n", 1)
595	            lines = rest.strip().splitlines()
596	            assert "user:2001:r--" in acl_part, f"ACL entry not preserved on pc2:\n{acl_part}"
597	            assert int(lines[0]) == _BACKDATED_MTIME, f"backdated mtime not preserved: {lines[0]}"
598	            assert lines[1] == lines[2], f"hard-link pair not sharing an inode on pc2 ({lines[1]} != {lines[2]})"
599	            assert lines[3] == "f644.txt", f"symlink target wrong on pc2: {lines[3]!r}"
600	
601	            # 3d. Exclusions: config-excluded subtree absent; ADR-016 runtime excludes held.
602	            excl = await pc2_executor.run_command(
603	                f"test ! -e {tree}/secret/token.txt", timeout=10.0, login_shell=False
604	            )
605	            assert excl.success, "Config-excluded secret/token.txt reached pc2 (exclusion failed)."
606	            runtime = await pc2_executor.run_command(
607	                f"test ! -e {_STATE_DIR}/SENTINEL_SOURCE && "
608	                f"test -e {_STATE_DIR}/SENTINEL_TARGET && "
609	                f"test -e ~/.local/bin/pc-switcher",
610	                timeout=10.0,
611	            )
612	            assert runtime.success, (
613	                "ADR-016 runtime exclusion failed: pc1's state reached pc2, or pc2's own state/install was "
614	                "clobbered by the --delete mirror of /home."
615	            )
616	
617	            # 3e. SC3 inclusion: non-excluded dev-tool cache + VS Code user state ARE synced,
618	            # while a config-excluded sibling (VS Code Cache) is not.
619	            marker_rels = list(_INCLUDED_MARKERS)
620	            inc = await pc2_executor.run_command(
621	                " && echo '|' && ".join(f"cat ~/{rel}" for rel in marker_rels)
622	                + f" && echo '|' && ( test ! -e ~/{_EXCLUDED_MARKER} && echo EXCLUDED_ABSENT )",
623	                timeout=10.0,
624	                login_shell=False,
625	            )
626	            assert inc.success, f"SC3 inclusion checks failed on pc2: {inc.stderr}"
627	            inc_parts = [p.strip() for p in inc.stdout.split("|")]
628	            for rel, part in zip(marker_rels, inc_parts, strict=False):
629	                assert part == _INCLUDED_MARKERS[rel], (
630	                    f"Included path {rel} not synced to pc2 (SC3): got {part!r}, want {_INCLUDED_MARKERS[rel]!r}"
631	                )
632	            assert "EXCLUDED_ABSENT" in inc_parts[-1], (
633	                f"Config-excluded {_EXCLUDED_MARKER} reached pc2 (SC3 exclusion failed)."
634	            )
635	
636	            # --- Step 4: mutate pc2, then B→A ---
637	            mutate = await pc2_executor.run_command(
638	                f"""set -e
639	T={tree}
640	printf 'added-on-pc2' > "$T/added.txt"; chmod 750 "$T/added.txt"; touch -d "@{_ADDITION_MTIME}" "$T/added.txt"
641	printf 'MODIFIED-644' > "$T/f644.txt"
642	rm -f "$T/f600.txt"
643	rm -rf "$T/d700"
644	chmod 700 "$T/f755.txt"
645	""",
646	                timeout=15.0,
647	                login_shell=False,
648	            )
649	            assert mutate.success, f"Mutation on pc2 failed: {mutate.stderr}"
650	
651	            sync_ba = await pc2_executor.run_command("pc-switcher sync pc1 --yes", timeout=300.0, login_shell=True)
652	            assert sync_ba.success, (
653	                f"B→A sync failed.\nexit={sync_ba.exit_code}\nstdout: {sync_ba.stdout}\nstderr: {sync_ba.stderr}"
654	            )
655	
656	            roundtrip = await pc1_executor.run_command(
657	                f"cat {tree}/added.txt && echo '|' && "
658	                f"stat -c '%a %Y' {tree}/added.txt && echo '|' && "
659	                f"cat {tree}/f644.txt && echo '|' && "
660	                f"stat -c '%a' {tree}/f755.txt && echo '|' && "
661	                f"( test ! -e {tree}/f600.txt && echo GONE_FILE ) && "
662	                f"( test ! -e {tree}/d700 && echo GONE_DIR )",
663	                timeout=15.0,
664	                login_shell=False,
665	            )
666	            assert roundtrip.success, f"pc1 checks after B→A failed: {roundtrip.stderr}"
667	            added_content, added_meta, f644_content, f755_mode, gone = [p.strip() for p in roundtrip.stdout.split("|")]
668	            assert added_content == "added-on-pc2", f"addition content wrong on pc1: {added_content!r}"
669	            added_mode, added_mtime = added_meta.split()
670	            assert added_mode == "750", f"addition perms not preserved on B→A: {added_mode}"
671	            assert int(added_mtime) == _ADDITION_MTIME, f"addition mtime not preserved: {added_mtime}"
672	            assert f644_content == "MODIFIED-644", f"modification not propagated on B→A: {f644_content!r}"
673	            assert f755_mode == "700", f"permission change not propagated on B→A: {f755_mode}"
674	            assert "GONE_FILE" in gone, "file deletion (f600.txt) not propagated on B→A"
675	            assert "GONE_DIR" in gone, "directory deletion (d700) not propagated on B→A"
676	
677	            # --- Step 5: clean A→B again must not trip the out-of-order gate ---
678	            sync_ab2 = await pc1_executor.run_command("pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes", timeout=300.0, login_shell=True)
679	            assert sync_ab2.success, (
680	                f"Second A→B failed (out-of-order gate wrongly tripped for a clean round-trip, ADR-015 #159).\n"
681	                f"exit={sync_ab2.exit_code}\nstdout: {sync_ab2.stdout}\nstderr: {sync_ab2.stderr}"
682	            )
683	
684	        finally:
685	            await _remove_test_artifacts(pc1_executor, pc2_executor, tree)
686	
687	    async def test_core_us_job_arch_as7_interrupt_terminates_job(
688	        self,
689	        sync_ready_source_long_duration: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
690	        pc2_executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
691	    ) -> None:
692	        """Test CORE-US-JOB-ARCH-AS7: Ctrl+C terminates job with cleanup.
693	
694	        Verifies that when user presses Ctrl+C during job execution, the orchestrator:
695	        - Catches SIGINT signal
696	        - Requests termination of currently-executing job
697	        - Logs interruption at WARNING level
698	        - Exits with code 130
699	
700	        Expected behavior:
701	        1. Start sync with long-running dummy_success job (60s)
702	        2. Wait for job to begin execution
703	        3. Send SIGINT to the sync process
704	        4. Verify process exits with code 130
705	        5. Verify "interrupted" message in output
706	
707	        Test approach:
708	        - Start sync in background using nohup and capture PID
709	        - Wait for sync to start (check for running process or log output)
710	        - Send SIGINT to the process
711	        - Wait for process to terminate
712	        - Check exit code and output
713	        """
714	        pc1_executor = sync_ready_source_long_duration
715	
716	        # Start sync in background and capture output to a temp file
717	        # Use script to run in a pseudo-terminal for proper signal handling
718	        output_file = "/tmp/pcswitcher-e2e-interrupt-test-output.txt"
719	        pid_file = "/tmp/pcswitcher-e2e-interrupt-test-pid.txt"
720	
721	        # Clean up from any previous run
722	        await pc1_executor.run_command(f"rm -f {output_file} {pid_file}", timeout=10.0)
723	
724	        # Start sync in background with script for TTY emulation
725	        # We use bash -c to wrap the command and capture the PID.
726	        # --allow-first-sync: pc2 has no sync history (W1 gate, ADR-015); required in CI
727	        # (no TTY) to bypass the first-sync overwrite confirmation and reach job execution.
728	        start_result = await pc1_executor.run_command(
729	            f"nohup bash -c 'echo $$ > {pid_file};"
730	            f" exec pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes --allow-first-sync 2>&1'"
731	            f" > {output_file} &",
732	            timeout=10.0,
733	            login_shell=True,
734	        )
735	        assert start_result.success, f"Failed to start background sync: {start_result.stderr}"
736	
737	        # Wait for PID file to be written and process to start
738	        await asyncio.sleep(2)
739	
740	        # Get the PID
741	        pid_result = await pc1_executor.run_command(f"cat {pid_file}", timeout=10.0)
742	        assert pid_result.success and pid_result.stdout.strip(), f"Failed to get sync process PID: {pid_result.stderr}"
743	        sync_pid = pid_result.stdout.strip()
744	
745	        # Wait for sync to actually start (look for connection or log activity)
746	        # Give it time to establish SSH connection and start job execution
747	        for _ in range(30):  # Wait up to 30 seconds for job to start
748	            await asyncio.sleep(1)
749	            output_check = await pc1_executor.run_command(f"cat {output_file} 2>/dev/null || true", timeout=10.0)
750	            # Check if we see any progress indicating sync has started
751	            if "source" in output_check.stdout.lower() or "target" in output_check.stdout.lower():
752	                break
753	            if "connecting" in output_check.stdout.lower() or "lock" in output_check.stdout.lower():
754	                continue  # Still in setup phase, keep waiting
755	            # Check if process is still running
756	            ps_check = await pc1_executor.run_command(f"ps -p {sync_pid} -o pid= 2>/dev/null || true", timeout=5.0)
757	            if not ps_check.stdout.strip():
758	                break  # Process finished (possibly errored out)
759	
760	        # Send SIGINT to the sync process
761	        await pc1_executor.run_command(
762	            f"kill -INT {sync_pid} 2>/dev/null || true",
763	            timeout=10.0,
764	            login_shell=False,
765	        )
766	
767	        # Wait for process to terminate (up to 35 seconds for cleanup timeout)
768	        process_terminated = False
769	        for _ in range(40):  # Wait up to 40 seconds
770	            await asyncio.sleep(1)
771	            ps_check = await pc1_executor.run_command(
772	                f"ps -p {sync_pid} -o pid= 2>/dev/null || echo 'terminated'",
773	                timeout=5.0,
774	                login_shell=False,
775	            )
776	            if "terminated" in ps_check.stdout or not ps_check.stdout.strip():
777	                process_terminated = True
778	                break
779	
780	        assert process_terminated, f"Sync process {sync_pid} did not terminate after SIGINT"
781	
782	        # Read the output
783	        output_result = await pc1_executor.run_command(f"cat {output_file}", timeout=10.0)
784	        output_text = output_result.stdout
785	
786	        # Verify interrupt handling message
787	        assert "interrupt" in output_text.lower(), f"Output should contain interrupt message.\nOutput:\n{output_text}"
788	
789	        # Clean up temp files
790	        await pc1_executor.run_command(f"rm -f {output_file} {pid_file}", timeout=10.0)

792	    async def test_core_edge_target_unreachable_mid_sync(
793	        self,
794	        pc1_with_pcswitcher_mod: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
795	        pc2_executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
796	        reset_pcswitcher_state: None,
797	        pc1_to_pc2_traffic_blocker: Pc1ToPc2TrafficBlocker,
798	    ) -> None:
799	        """Test CORE-EDGE: Target becomes unreachable mid-sync.
800	
801	        Spec reference: docs/system/spec.md - Edge Cases
802	
803	        Simulates network failure by blocking pc1→pc2 traffic with iptables
804	        during the target phase of DummySuccessJob. Verifies that:
805	        - Sync detects the connection failure
806	        - Sync exits with non-zero code
807	        - Error output indicates connection/network failure
808	
809	        Test approach:
810	        1. Configure DummySuccessJob with short source phase (4s) and longer target (30s)
811	        2. Start sync in background, capturing output to temp file
812	        3. Monitor output for "target phase" indicator
813	        4. When detected, block pc1→pc2 traffic via iptables
814	        5. Wait for sync to fail (keepalive timeout ~45s)
815	        6. Verify error message indicates connection failure
816	
817	        Safety:
818	        - iptables rule only blocks pc1→pc2, test runner retains full access
819	        - network_blocker fixture ensures cleanup even on test failure
820	        """
821	        _ = reset_pcswitcher_state  # Ensures test isolation
822	        pc1_executor = pc1_with_pcswitcher_mod
823	
824	        # Create test config with short source phase but longer target phase
825	        # Source: 4s (quick to get to target phase)
826	        # Target: 30s (long enough for us to inject failure and observe timeout)
827	        test_config = _TEST_CONFIG_TEMPLATE.format(source_duration=4, target_duration=30)
828	        await pc1_executor.run_command("mkdir -p ~/.config/pc-switcher", timeout=10.0)
829	        await pc1_executor.run_command(
830	            f"cat > ~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml << 'EOF'\n{test_config}EOF",
831	            timeout=10.0,
832	        )
833	
834	        # Start sync in background, capturing output to temp file
835	        output_file = "/tmp/pcswitcher-network-failure-test-output.txt"
836	        pid_file = "/tmp/pcswitcher-network-failure-test-pid.txt"
837	        await pc1_executor.run_command(f"rm -f {output_file} {pid_file}", timeout=10.0)
838	
839	        # Start sync in background.
840	        # --allow-first-sync: pc2 has no sync history (W1 gate, ADR-015); required in CI
841	        # (no TTY) to bypass the first-sync overwrite confirmation so the sync proceeds
842	        # into the job execution phase where the network failure is injected.
843	        start_result = await pc1_executor.run_command(
844	            f"nohup bash -c 'echo $$ > {pid_file};"
845	            f" exec pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes --allow-first-sync 2>&1'"
846	            f" > {output_file} &",
847	            timeout=10.0,
848	            login_shell=True,
849	        )
850	        assert start_result.success, f"Failed to start background sync: {start_result.stderr}"
851	
852	        # Wait for PID file and get PID
853	        await asyncio.sleep(2)
854	        pid_result = await pc1_executor.run_command(f"cat {pid_file}", timeout=10.0)
855	        assert pid_result.success and pid_result.stdout.strip(), f"Failed to get sync process PID: {pid_result.stderr}"
856	        sync_pid = pid_result.stdout.strip()
857	
858	        # Monitor log file for "Target phase:" indicator, then block network
859	        # The TUI "Recent Logs" only shows FULL level messages, but DummySuccessJob
860	        # logs at INFO level. We check the log file directly for reliable detection.
861	        network_blocked = False
862	        last_log_content = ""
863	        for _ in range(60):  # Wait up to 60 seconds for target phase
864	            await asyncio.sleep(1)
865	
866	            # Check the log file for "Target phase:" messages
867	            log_check = await pc1_executor.run_command(
868	                "cat ~/.local/share/pc-switcher/logs/sync-*.log 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'target phase' || true",
869	                timeout=10.0,
870	            )
871	            last_log_content = log_check.stdout
872	
873	            # Check if target phase has started
874	            if "target phase" in last_log_content.lower():
875	                # Block pc1→pc2 traffic
876	                await pc1_to_pc2_traffic_blocker.block()
877	                network_blocked = True
878	                break
879	
880	            # Check if process is still running
881	            ps_check = await pc1_executor.run_command(
882	                f"ps -p {sync_pid} -o pid= 2>/dev/null || true",
883	                timeout=5.0,
884	                login_shell=False,
885	            )
886	            if not ps_check.stdout.strip():
887	                break  # Process exited early
888	
889	        # Read TUI output for debugging if assertion fails
890	        tui_output = await pc1_executor.run_command(
891	            f"cat {output_file} 2>/dev/null || true",
892	            timeout=10.0,
893	        )
894	
895	        assert network_blocked, (
896	            f"Target phase not detected before process exited.\n"
897	            f"Log content:\n{last_log_content}\n"
898	            f"TUI output:\n{tui_output.stdout}"
899	        )
900	
901	        # Wait for sync to fail due to keepalive timeout (~45 seconds)
902	        # Total wait: up to 90 seconds to be safe
903	        process_exited = False
904	        for _ in range(90):
905	            await asyncio.sleep(1)
906	            ps_check = await pc1_executor.run_command(
907	                f"ps -p {sync_pid} -o pid= 2>/dev/null || echo 'exited'",
908	                timeout=5.0,
909	                login_shell=False,
910	            )
911	            if "exited" in ps_check.stdout or not ps_check.stdout.strip():
912	                process_exited = True
913	                break
914	
915	        assert process_exited, f"Sync process {sync_pid} did not exit after network failure"
916	
917	        # Read final output
918	        output_result = await pc1_executor.run_command(f"cat {output_file}", timeout=10.0)
919	        output_text = output_result.stdout
920	
921	        # Verify sync failed with connection-related error
922	        # Look for various error indicators
923	        error_indicators = [
924	            "connection",
925	            "timeout",
926	            "unreachable",
927	            "lost",
928	            "closed",
929	            "failed",
930	            "error",
931	            "ssh",
932	        ]
933	        output_lower = output_text.lower()
934	        has_error_indicator = any(ind in output_lower for ind in error_indicators)
935	
936	        assert has_error_indicator, f"Output should indicate connection failure.\nOutput:\n{output_text}"
937	
938	        # Clean up temp files
939	        await pc1_executor.run_command(f"rm -f {output_file} {pid_file}", timeout=10.0)
940

1078	class TestConsecutiveSyncWarning:
1079	    """Integration tests for first-sync (W1) gate and consecutive-push (W3) warning (ADR-015).
1080	
1081	    Tests verify that:
1082	    - Sync history is updated on both source and target after successful sync
1083	    - First sync to a target with no history (W1) is gated by --allow-first-sync
1084	    - Consecutive syncs without back-sync (W3) are blocked (non-interactive, defaults to abort)
1085	    - --allow-out-of-order flag bypasses the W3 consecutive-push gate
1086	    - Back-sync workflow clears the consecutive-push warning state
1087	    """
1088	
1089	    async def test_consecutive_sync_warning_workflow(
1090	        self,
1091	        sync_ready_source: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
1092	        pc2_executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
1093	    ) -> None:
1094	        """Test first-sync (W1) gate and consecutive-push (W3) warning workflow.
1095	
1096	        TODO add links to the Semantic IDs for ALL tests executed here.
1097	        TODO change name of test to something that covers everything done here.
1098	
1099	        Consolidated test covering:
1100	        - First sync to a fresh target (W1): gated by --allow-first-sync; sync history
1101	          updated on both machines after success.
1102	        - Consecutive push (W3): second A→B without a back-sync is blocked in non-interactive
1103	          mode (no flag, defaults to abort).
1104	        - --allow-out-of-order bypasses the W3 consecutive-push gate.
1105	
1106	        Workflow:
1107	        1. First sync with --allow-first-sync → verifies W1 gate passed, history updated
1108	        2. Second sync (no flag) → verifies blocked by W3 gate (consecutive push)
1109	        3. Third sync with --allow-out-of-order → verifies W3 gate bypassed
1110	
1111	        This consolidation saves ~2 sync operations (~16 seconds) compared
1112	        to running these as separate tests.
1113	        """
1114	        pc1_executor = sync_ready_source
1115	
1116	        # History cleanup done by reset_pcswitcher_state fixture (via sync_ready_source)
1117	
1118	        # Step 1: First sync (W1 gate) — pc2 has no history; --allow-first-sync is required
1119	        # in non-interactive CI to bypass the first-sync overwrite confirmation.
1120	        first_sync = await pc1_executor.run_command(
1121	            "pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes --allow-first-sync",
1122	            timeout=180.0,
1123	            login_shell=True,
1124	        )
1125	        assert first_sync.success, (
1126	            f"First sync failed.\nExit code: {first_sync.exit_code}\n"
1127	            f"Stdout: {first_sync.stdout}\nStderr: {first_sync.stderr}"
1128	        )
1129	
1130	        # Verify source history
1131	        pc1_history = await pc1_executor.run_command(
1132	            "cat ~/.local/share/pc-switcher/sync-history.json",
1133	            timeout=10.0,
1134	        )
1135	        assert pc1_history.success, f"Failed to read pc1 history: {pc1_history.stderr}"
1136	        assert '"last_role": "source"' in pc1_history.stdout, (
1137	            f"pc1 should have last_role=source.\nContent: {pc1_history.stdout}"
1138	        )
1139	
1140	        # Verify target history
1141	        pc2_history = await pc2_executor.run_command(
1142	            "cat ~/.local/share/pc-switcher/sync-history.json",
1143	            timeout=10.0,
1144	        )
1145	        assert pc2_history.success, f"Failed to read pc2 history: {pc2_history.stderr}"
1146	        assert '"last_role": "target"' in pc2_history.stdout, (
1147	            f"pc2 should have last_role=target.\nContent: {pc2_history.stdout}"
1148	        )
1149	
1150	        # Step 2: Second sync WITHOUT --allow-out-of-order — W3 (consecutive push) gate fires
1151	        # because pc1 is pushing to pc2 again without a back-sync.  Non-interactive mode
1152	        # cannot confirm, so it aborts (title: "Consecutive Sync — No Back-Sync Received").
1153	        second_sync = await pc1_executor.run_command(
1154	            "pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes",
1155	            timeout=60.0,
1156	            login_shell=True,
1157	        )
1158	        assert not second_sync.success, (
1159	            f"Second sync should fail (W3 consecutive-push gate, defaults to abort).\n"
1160	            f"Exit code: {second_sync.exit_code}\nStdout: {second_sync.stdout}"
1161	        )
1162	        output = second_sync.stdout + second_sync.stderr
1163	        # "consecutive" from "Consecutive Sync — No Back-Sync Received" (W3 warning title);
1164	        # "abort" from "Sync aborted at the out-of-order / target-state check" (RuntimeError).
1165	        assert "consecutive" in output.lower() and "abort" in output.lower(), (
1166	            f"Output should mention consecutive-push warning and abort.\nOutput: {output}"
1167	        )
1168	
1169	        # Step 3: Third sync WITH --allow-out-of-order bypasses the W3 gate.
1170	        third_sync = await pc1_executor.run_command(
1171	            "pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes --allow-out-of-order",
1172	            timeout=180.0,
1173	            login_shell=True,
1174	        )
1175	        assert third_sync.success, (
1176	            f"Third sync with --allow-out-of-order should succeed.\n"
1177	            f"Exit code: {third_sync.exit_code}\nStderr: {third_sync.stderr}"
1178	        )

1180	    async def test_back_sync_clears_warning(
1181	        self,
1182	        sync_ready_source: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
1183	        pc2_with_pcswitcher: BashLoginRemoteExecutor,
1184	    ) -> None:
1185	        """After receiving a back-sync, machine can sync again without warning.
1186	
1187	        Full workflow:
1188	        1. pc1 syncs to pc2 (W1: first-sync, --allow-first-sync required) → pc1=source, pc2=target
1189	        2. pc2 syncs back to pc1 (clean case: pc1 has history, target_peer=pc2==source)
1190	        3. pc1 syncs to pc2 again → should succeed WITHOUT --allow-out-of-order
1191	           because pc1 was last a target (received back-sync from pc2 = clean case)
1192	
1193	        NOTE: pc2_with_pcswitcher is used instead of pc2_executor to ensure
1194	        pc2 has the exact same version as pc1 (from current branch), which is
1195	        required for back-sync version validation to pass.
1196	        """
1197	        pc1_executor = sync_ready_source
1198	        pc2_executor = pc2_with_pcswitcher
1199	
1200	        # History cleanup done by reset_pcswitcher_state fixture (via sync_ready_source)
1201	
1202	        # Step 1: pc1 syncs to pc2 — W1 gate (pc2 has no history), --allow-first-sync required.
1203	        first_sync = await pc1_executor.run_command(
1204	            "pc-switcher sync pc2 --yes --allow-first-sync",
1205	            timeout=180.0,

content.text:
This is a substantial, correctness-sensitive extension to a real VM test. I've mapped the existing structure (strict-manifest `_TESTTREE`, marker-based inclusion checks, the single first-sync in Step 3). My plan: add a **separate** `pcsw-filter` scenario tree (kept out of the strict manifest), seed it distinctly on source and target, ride it on the existing Step-3 A→B sync, and assert every case. Let me reason through the rsync semantics once, then implement.

Key `--delete` + filter facts I'm relying on (verified earlier against rsync 3.2.7): an excluded path is invisible to the deletion scan, so a target-only excluded file **survives** (no `--delete-excluded`); an included subtree is subject to `--delete`, so a target-only file there is **removed**; per-dir merge rules are read from the *sender* and govern both transfer and deletion.

Let me implement. First, `import shlex`:

input.new_string:
# Sibling of Code/User that IS excluded by config — proves inclusion is selective.
_EXCLUDED_MARKER = ".config/Code/Cache/pcsw-cache-marker.bin"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# #166 filter-rule scenario (separate from the strict-manifest _TESTTREE).
#
# Rides on the same first A→B sync. Exercises, through the REAL rsync command
# built by _build_rsync_cmd, every filter surface:
#   - central include-override (keep pcsw-filter/cache/keep-uv + keep-pip, drop the rest);
#   - a wholly-excluded central subtree (pcsw-filter/excluded);
#   - nested per-directory .pcswitcher-filter files (pcsw-filter/nest and .../nest/deep);
# and verifies BOTH directions of correctness:
#   - included paths ADD / OVERWRITE / DELETE on the target (rsync --delete within);
#   - excluded paths leave the target AS-IS, both when a conflicting copy exists on the
#     source AND when the path exists ONLY on the target (the --delete survival case —
#     the important difference: no --delete-excluded, so excluded target files are never
#     removed even with no source counterpart);
#   - per-directory filter files themselves transfer to the target (no `e` modifier).
#
# This tree lives outside _TESTTREE so its intentional source/target divergences do not
# perturb the strict ownership/content manifest equality asserted for _TESTTREE.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_FILTER_TREE = "pcsw-filter"

# Files present on the SOURCE (pc1), home-relative → exact content.
_FILTER_SRC: dict[str, str] = {
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/add_me.txt": "src-add",  # source-only, included → added on target
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/overwrite_me.txt": "src-new",  # differs on target → overwrites it
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/keep_me.txt": "same",  # identical on target → unchanged
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/keep-uv/tool.txt": "uv-src",  # include-override kept subfolder
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/keep-pip/tool.txt": "pip-src",  # include-override kept subfolder
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/junk/blob.txt": "junk-src",  # dropped sibling → must not transfer
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/on_both.txt": "src-version",  # excluded → must not overwrite target
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/keep.txt": "nest-keep-src",  # per-dir subtree, not excluded → synced
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/nested_secret.txt": "src-secret",  # per-dir excluded → must not transfer
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/keep.txt": "deep-keep-src",  # nested, not excluded → synced
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/nested_secret.txt": "src-deep-secret",  # inherited exclude → no transfer
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/deep_only.txt": "src-deep-only",  # deep per-dir exclude → no transfer
}

# Per-directory filter files on the SOURCE (transfer to target; govern their subtree).
_FILTER_SRC_PERDIR: dict[str, str] = {
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/.pcswitcher-filter": "- nested_secret.txt",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/.pcswitcher-filter": "- deep_only.txt",
}

# Files PRE-EXISTING on the TARGET (pc2) before the first sync (drive the --delete cases).
_FILTER_TGT: dict[str, str] = {
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/overwrite_me.txt": "tgt-old",  # included → overwritten by source
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/keep_me.txt": "same",  # identical → untouched
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/delete_me.txt": "tgt-doomed",  # included, source-absent → deleted
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/junk/tgt_junk.txt": "tgt-junk",  # excluded, source-absent → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/on_both.txt": "tgt-version",  # excluded, source-present → not overwritten
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/tgt_only.txt": "tgt-survivor",  # excluded, source-absent → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/nested_secret.txt": "tgt-secret",  # per-dir excluded → not overwritten/deleted
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/nested_secret.txt": "tgt-deep-secret",  # inherited exclude → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/deep_only.txt": "tgt-deep",  # deep per-dir exclude → survives
}

# Expected TARGET state after the first A→B sync (None ⇒ must be absent).
_FILTER_EXPECT: dict[str, str | None] = {
    # Included subtree: add / overwrite / keep / delete.
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/add_me.txt": "src-add",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/overwrite_me.txt": "src-new",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/keep_me.txt": "same",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/delete_me.txt": None,
    # Include-override (#166): kept dev caches sync; the dropped sibling never arrives.
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/keep-uv/tool.txt": "uv-src",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/keep-pip/tool.txt": "pip-src",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/junk/blob.txt": None,
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/junk/tgt_junk.txt": "tgt-junk",
    # Central exclusion protects the target both ways (conflicting copy, and target-only).
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/on_both.txt": "tgt-version",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/tgt_only.txt": "tgt-survivor",
    # Nested per-directory filters: the files transfer; their rules protect the target subtree.
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/.pcswitcher-filter": "- nested_secret.txt",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/.pcswitcher-filter": "- deep_only.txt",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/keep.txt": "nest-keep-src",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/keep.txt": "deep-keep-src",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/nested_secret.txt": "tgt-secret",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/nested_secret.txt": "tgt-deep-secret",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/deep_only.txt": "tgt-deep",
}


def _tree(user: str) -> str:
    """Absolute path of the seeded rich test subtree within the real home."""
    return f"/home/{user}/{_TESTTREE}"

input.new_string:
def _make_e2e_home_filter() -> str:
    """Central `merge` filter for the /home folder_sync entry (#166).

    Exercises, end-to-end through the real rsync command, every central filter surface:
    the machine-identity/regenerable excludes (as before); the #166 include-override idiom
    (keep the dev caches under pcsw-filter/cache while dropping the rest, via `+` re-includes
    ordered before a `-` on the parent's children — the exact ancestor-descent shape the
    shipped home.filter uses); and a wholly-excluded subtree (pcsw-filter/excluded). The
    per-directory .pcswitcher-filter files seeded under pcsw-filter/nest are activated by the
    job's own always-emitted `dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter` rule, not by this file. Patterns
    are floating (no leading /), matching the shipped home.filter, because /home syncs with
    each user's directory one level below the transfer root.
    """
    return f"""\
- .ssh/id_*
- .ssh/known_hosts
- .config/tailscale
- .config/Code/Cache
- .config/Code/CachedData
- .config/Code/GPUCache
- .cache
- .local/share/uv/python
+ {_FILTER_TREE}/cache/
+ {_FILTER_TREE}/cache/keep-uv/***
+ {_FILTER_TREE}/cache/keep-pip/***
- {_FILTER_TREE}/cache/*
- {_FILTER_TREE}/excluded
- {_TESTTREE}/secret
"""

input.new_string:
result = await executor.run_command(cmd, timeout=10.0)
    assert result.success, f"Failed to write filter file: {result.stderr}"


def _seed_files_script(files: dict[str, str]) -> str:
    """Build a `set -e` shell script writing each home-relative path with its exact content.

    `.pcswitcher-filter` files get a trailing newline (rsync merge-file convention); every
    other file is written with `printf %s` (no trailing newline) so an exact content
    comparison on the target is unambiguous.
    """
    lines = ["set -e"]
    for rel, content in files.items():
        fmt = r"'%s\n'" if os.path.basename(rel) == ".pcswitcher-filter" else "%s"
        lines.append(f"mkdir -p ~/{os.path.dirname(rel)}")
        lines.append(f"printf {fmt} {shlex.quote(content)} > ~/{rel}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


async def _seed_filter_source(executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor) -> None:
    """Seed the #166 filter-scenario files (incl. per-directory filter files) on the source."""
    script = _seed_files_script({**_FILTER_SRC, **_FILTER_SRC_PERDIR})
    result = await executor.run_command(script, timeout=30.0, login_shell=False)
    assert result.success, f"Failed to seed filter-scenario source files: {result.stderr}"


async def _seed_filter_target(executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor) -> None:
    """Seed the pre-existing target-side files that the #166 filter assertions check against."""
    result = await executor.run_command(_seed_files_script(_FILTER_TGT), timeout=30.0, login_shell=False)
    assert result.success, f"Failed to seed filter-scenario target files: {result.stderr}"


async def _assert_filter_outcomes(executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor) -> None:
    """Assert the target's #166 filter tree matches `_FILTER_EXPECT` after the first sync.

    One command probes every expected path, emitting `<path>@@F@@<content-or-__ABSENT__>@@R@@`
    records (printable separators, robust to any file content); the parsed results are then
    compared here so a single assertion reports all discrepancies at once.
    """
    probe = "\n".join(
        f"printf %s {shlex.quote(rel)}; printf '@@F@@'; "
        f"if [ -e ~/{rel} ]; then cat ~/{rel}; else printf %s __ABSENT__; fi; printf '@@R@@'"
        for rel in _FILTER_EXPECT
    )
    result = await executor.run_command(probe, timeout=30.0, login_shell=False)
    assert result.success, f"filter-outcome probe failed on target: {result.stderr}"

    got: dict[str, str] = {}
    for record in result.stdout.split("@@R@@"):
        if not record:
            continue
        path, _, content = record.partition("@@F@@")
        got[path] = content

    errors: list[str] = []
    for rel, expected in _FILTER_EXPECT.items():
        actual = got.get(rel)
        if expected is None:
            if actual != "__ABSENT__":
                errors.append(f"{rel}: expected ABSENT on target, got {actual!r}")
        elif actual is None or actual == "__ABSENT__":
            errors.append(f"{rel}: expected {expected!r}, but the file is ABSENT on target")
        elif actual.strip() != expected.strip():
            errors.append(f"{rel}: expected {expected!r}, got {actual!r}")
    assert not errors, "Filter-rule outcomes on target are wrong:\n" + "\n".join(errors)


async def _seed_rich_tree(executor: BashLoginRemoteExecutor, tree: str) -> None:

input.content:
"""Reproduce the e2e #166 filter scenario against the REAL rsync binary, locally.

Imports the exact data dicts + central-filter builder from the integration test so
there is zero drift, lays down source/target trees under an `alice/` level (matching
/home syncing with contents as the transfer root, users one level below), runs rsync
with the same GLOBAL-FIRST filter order _build_rsync_cmd uses (merge before dir-merge),
then checks the resulting target tree against _FILTER_EXPECT.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import importlib.util
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path, PurePosixPath

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("e2e", "tests/integration/test_end_to_end_sync.py")
assert spec and spec.loader
mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(mod)

USER = "alice"  # stand-in for the per-user dir one level below the /home transfer root


def write(base: Path, rel: str, content: str, *, newline: bool) -> None:
    p = base / USER / rel
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(content + ("\n" if newline else ""))


def main() -> int:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
        root = Path(td)
        src = root / "src"
        dst = root / "dst"
        src.mkdir()
        dst.mkdir()

        for rel, content in mod._FILTER_SRC.items():
            write(src, rel, content, newline=False)
        for rel, content in mod._FILTER_SRC_PERDIR.items():
            write(src, rel, content, newline=True)
        for rel, content in mod._FILTER_TGT.items():
            write(dst, rel, content, newline=False)

        central = root / "home.filter"
        central.write_text(mod._make_e2e_home_filter())

        # Mirror _build_rsync_cmd: merge (central) BEFORE dir-merge (global-first).
        cmd = [
            "rsync",
            "-a",
            "--delete",
            f"--filter=merge {central}",
            "--filter=dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter",
            f"{src}/",
            f"{dst}/",
        ]
        proc = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
        if proc.returncode != 0:
            print("rsync FAILED:", proc.stderr)
            return 2

        errors: list[str] = []
        for rel, expected in mod._FILTER_EXPECT.items():
            path = dst / USER / rel
            if expected is None:
                if path.exists():
                    errors.append(f"{rel}: expected ABSENT, but present with {path.read_text()!r}")
            elif not path.exists():
                errors.append(f"{rel}: expected {expected!r}, but ABSENT")
            elif path.read_text().strip() != expected.strip():
                errors.append(f"{rel}: expected {expected!r}, got {path.read_text()!r}")

        if errors:
            print(f"MISMATCH ({len(errors)}):")
            for e in errors:
                print("  -", e)
            return 1
        print(f"OK: all {len(mod._FILTER_EXPECT)} expected target outcomes match real rsync.")
        # Also surface what rsync actually did, for the record.
        print("--- rsync itemized (real run) ---")
        return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

content.text: Important — my assumption was wrong. A `dir-merge` exclude does **not** protect a pre-existing target file from `--delete` (secret.env was deleted). This is a subtle and safety-relevant rsync behavior. Let me pin down exactly when per-dir excludes protect vs delete:

content.text:
Now I understand the precise, safety-relevant rsync behavior:

- **Central (`merge`) excludes protect target files from `--delete`** — whether the file is target-only (EXP3) or on both (EXP4). My `excluded/*` cases used the central filter and **passed** in the repro. ✓
- **Per-dir (`dir-merge`) excludes protect a pre-existing target file only if the `.pcswitcher-filter` is already on the receiver** (EXP2 survived; EXP1 deleted). On a first sync where the target lacks the filter file, a per-dir-excluded target file **gets deleted** — like how `.gitignore` doesn't protect already-present files it didn't cover before.

That's a genuine finding, and it's exactly why the issue chose global-first (central filter for enforced protection). My original expectations were wrong. Let me fix the per-dir scenario to pre-seed the filter files on the target (the realistic steady-state, where protection works) and fix the overwrite case (it was same-size/same-second, so rsync's quick-check skipped it):

input.old_string:

    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/keep_me.txt": "same",  # identical → untouched
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/delete_me.txt": "tgt-doomed",  # included, source-absent → deleted
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/junk/tgt_junk.txt": "tgt-junk",  # excluded, source-absent → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/on_both.txt": "tgt-version",  # excluded, source-present → not overwritten
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/tgt_only.txt": "tgt-survivor",  # excluded, source-absent → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/nested_secret.txt": "tgt-secret",  # per-dir excluded → not overwritten/deleted
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/nested_secret.txt": "tgt-deep-secret",  # inherited exclude → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/deep_only.txt": "tgt-deep",  # deep per-dir exclude → survives
}
input.new_string:
# Files PRE-EXISTING on the TARGET (pc2) before the first sync (drive the --delete cases).
# The `overwrite_me` target copy differs in SIZE from the source's, not just content, so
# rsync's default (size, mtime) quick-check always transfers it regardless of seed timing.
# The per-directory .pcswitcher-filter files are pre-seeded on the target too: a dir-merge
# exclude protects a pre-existing target file from --delete ONLY when the filter file is
# already present on the receiver during its deletion scan (verified against rsync 3.2.7).
# Without it on the target, a per-dir-excluded target file is DELETED on the first sync —
# like .gitignore not protecting a path it didn't cover before. Central-filter excludes
# (the pcsw-filter/excluded cases above) protect the target unconditionally; per-dir files
# reach parity only once they exist on both ends (the steady state modelled here).
_FILTER_TGT: dict[str, str] = {
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/overwrite_me.txt": "old",  # included, differing size → overwritten by source
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/keep_me.txt": "same",  # identical → untouched
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/synced/delete_me.txt": "tgt-doomed",  # included, source-absent → deleted
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/cache/junk/tgt_junk.txt": "tgt-junk",  # central-excluded, source-absent → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/on_both.txt": "tgt-version",  # central-excluded, source-present → not overwritten
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/excluded/tgt_only.txt": "tgt-survivor",  # central-excluded, source-absent → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/.pcswitcher-filter": "- nested_secret.txt",  # present on target → protection active
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/.pcswitcher-filter": "- deep_only.txt",
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/nested_secret.txt": "tgt-secret",  # per-dir excluded → not overwritten/deleted
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/nested_secret.txt": "tgt-deep-secret",  # inherited exclude → survives
    f"{_FILTER_TREE}/nest/deep/deep_only.txt": "tgt-deep",  # deep per-dir exclude → survives
}

content.text: All green. Now this finding — that a per-dir `.pcswitcher-filter` exclude doesn't protect a pre-existing target file from `--delete` until the filter file itself exists on the target — is a real, safety-relevant nuance the docs don't yet cover. Let me add a concise note to the configuration guide.

result.output:
120	      filter_file: ~/.config/pc-switcher/root.filter
121	```
122	
123	Each folder mirrors `path` (`rsync --delete`) minus the paths matched by its filter rules. Include-override is possible: you can exclude a subtree and re-include selected children of it (e.g. drop all of `~/.cache` but keep `~/.cache/uv`), because pc-switcher passes native rsync filter syntax straight through.
124	
125	Setting `enabled: false` skips a folder. Excluded files are never deleted on the target (`--delete-excluded` is deliberately not used), so a machine-specific file such as `.ssh/id_ed25519` survives on both ends.
126	
127	### Filter rules
128	
129	Two authoring surfaces are available per folder, both native rsync filter syntax (`+ pattern` include, `- pattern` exclude, first-match-wins):
130	
131	- **`filter_file`** — a per-folder central filter file, spliced in via rsync `merge`. `pc-switcher init` ships `home.filter` and `root.filter` next to `config.yaml`; edit them directly.
132	- **`.pcswitcher-filter`** — a per-directory file, spliced in tree-wide via rsync `dir-merge`. Drop one anywhere in the synced tree and it takes effect for that subtree, inheriting into deeper directories. `.pcswitcher-filter` files themselves sync to the target, like a committed `.gitignore`. This is pc-switcher's own filename — it is **not** rsync's built-in `.rsync-filter`, which pc-switcher never activates (no `-F`/`-FF`/`-C`/`--cvs-exclude` are ever passed, so a stray `.rsync-filter` in a synced tree has no effect).
133	
134	Precedence is **GLOBAL-FIRST**: pc-switcher's own runtime-file excludes (below) are evaluated first and can never be overridden; the folder's central `filter_file` is evaluated next, before any `.pcswitcher-filter`. Because rsync is first-match-wins, a central `-` rule always beats a later per-directory `+` rule — central policy is authoritative and a per-directory file can never re-expose something the central filter excluded.
135	
136	A configured `filter_file` that does not exist on the source fails validation before any transfer runs (fail-fast) — a missing filter file never silently degrades to a full unfiltered mirror.
137	
138	Core syntax, applied relative to the folder's transfer root (for `path: /home`, the root is the *contents* of `/home`, so `.ssh/id_*` matches `<user>/.ssh/id_*` for every user):
139	
140	- A pattern **containing a `/`** (other than a trailing one) or `**` is matched against the full path; a pattern with **no `/`** is matched against the final path component only. So `nvidia` excludes any file or directory named `nvidia` at any depth, while `.cache/nvidia` matches the two-element path ending.
141	- A pattern **without a leading `/`** floats — it matches at the *end* of the path, i.e. at any depth. `.cache/nvidia` therefore matches both `.cache/nvidia` and `alice/.cache/nvidia`.
142	- A pattern **with a leading `/`** is anchored to the transfer root. `/lost+found` matches only at the top of the synced folder.
143	- A **trailing `/`** restricts the match to directories: `foo/` excludes only a directory named `foo`.
144	- Wildcards: `?` matches one non-slash character; `*` matches zero or more non-slash characters (stops at `/`); `**` matches across `/`; `[…]` is a character class; a trailing `/***` matches a directory and all its contents.
145	
146	Examples:
147	
148	| Pattern | Matches |
149	|---------|---------|
150	| `.ssh/id_*` | `id_*` files inside any `.ssh` directory at any depth |
151	| `.config/tailscale` | a `tailscale` entry inside any `.config` directory |
152	| `.cache/nvidia` | a `nvidia` entry inside any `.cache` directory |
153	| `/lost+found` | `lost+found` only at the folder root |
154	| `*.tmp` | any file ending in `.tmp` at any depth |
155	| `node_modules/` | any directory named `node_modules` |
156	
157	For the full specification (all wildcard forms, anchoring, merge files, modifiers), see the rsync manpage "FILTER RULES" / "INCLUDE/EXCLUDE PATTERN RULES" section: https://download.samba.org/pub/rsync/rsync.1
158	
159	### Coming from .gitignore
160	
161	rsync's filter rules resemble `.gitignore` but differ in a few load-bearing ways:
162	
163	- **Signs, not `!`**: rsync uses `+`/`-` prefixes instead of gitignore's bare pattern / `!`-negated pattern. A `.pcswitcher-filter` with only `-` lines behaves like an ordinary `.gitignore`.
164	- **First-match-wins, the opposite of gitignore's last-match-wins.** In rsync, the *first* rule that matches a path decides it; later rules never override an earlier match. In gitignore, later lines override earlier ones.
165	- **A leading `/` anchors to the transfer root; a middle slash does not anchor.** `/a/direct` matches only the root's `a/direct`, while `a/direct` (slash present but not leading) matches `a/direct` at *any* depth. This differs from gitignore, where a middle slash anchors the pattern to the `.gitignore`'s own directory.
166	- **A trailing `/` means directory-only** — same as gitignore.
167	- **A no-slash pattern matches the final path component at any depth** — like a gitignore basename pattern (e.g. `nvidia` matches both `.cache/nvidia` and `alice/.cache/nvidia`).
168	- **`a/**/b` does not match `a/b`.** `**` requires at least the slash-structure it spans, so the zero-intermediate-directory case is not covered; use `a/b` (or `a/**b`) if you also need that case.
169	- In `dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter`, the leading `/` only means the filename has no path component (it is a bare name) — it does **not** restrict the file to the root. The dir-merge rule is honored in *any* directory of the tree, exactly like `.gitignore` is honored in any directory that has one.

content.text: Let me add the per-dir `--delete` nuance to the Filter rules section (the natural home, right after the fail-fast paragraph):

input.old_string: A configured `filter_file` that does not exist on the source fails validation before any transfer runs (fail-fast) — a missing filter file never silently degrades to a full unfiltered mirror.
input.new_string:
A configured `filter_file` that does not exist on the source fails validation before any transfer runs (fail-fast) — a missing filter file never silently degrades to a full unfiltered mirror.

On a `--delete` mirror the two surfaces differ in one safety-relevant way. The central `filter_file` is passed to rsync on every run, so a `-` rule in it protects a matching file that already exists on the target from deletion immediately — even on the very first sync, and whether or not the source has a counterpart. A per-directory `.pcswitcher-filter` protects a pre-existing target file from deletion only once that filter file is itself present on the target: its rules are read from the source side, so on a first sync to a target that does not yet have the `.pcswitcher-filter`, a target file the rule would exclude is still deleted — the same way adding a line to `.gitignore` does not retroactively untrack a file. The `.pcswitcher-filter` transfers like any other file, so from the next sync onward the exclusion protects the target. For rules that must never delete or expose something (secrets, machine-specific state), prefer the central `filter_file`.
