content.text:
Done. Pushed to the draft branch (PR #173 confirmed still draft — no VM suite triggered).

## What changed

Extended the `/home` VM e2e test (`test_core_us_job_arch_as1...`) to exercise the **whole filter surface** through the real rsync-over-SSH command, on a dedicated `pcsw-filter` tree kept out of the strict metadata manifest so its intentional source/target divergences don't perturb the existing assertions. Coverage now maps to your list:

| Your requirement | How it's tested |
|---|---|
| Global filter file | Central `merge` with the `.cache`-style **include-override** (`+ keep-uv/keep-pip`, `- cache/*`) — the shipped `home.filter` shape |
| Per-dir filter, nested | `nest/.pcswitcher-filter` + `nest/deep/.pcswitcher-filter`, with **inheritance** into `deep/` |
| Includes + excludes + #166 misc | The cache include-override + a plain synced subtree + a wholly-excluded subtree |
| Included → deletes/overwrites/adds on target | `add_me` (added), `overwrite_me` (overwritten), `delete_me` (target-only → **deleted** by `--delete`) |
| Excluded → target as-is, source-exists case | `excluded/on_both.txt` stays `tgt-version` (source copy not transferred) |
| Excluded → target as-is, **nothing on source** | `excluded/tgt_only.txt` survives `--delete` (central-excluded, no source counterpart) |
| Per-dir files transferred | `.pcswitcher-filter` files assert-present on target |

## The important finding (verified against real rsync 3.2.7)

I couldn't run the VMs, so I **reproduced the exact scenario locally against the real rsync binary** (same filter file, per-dir files, global-first order that `_build_rsync_cmd` emits). The first run failed 4 assertions — two were real semantics I had wrong:

1. **A per-directory `.pcswitcher-filter` exclude does NOT protect a pre-existing target file from `--delete` until the filter file itself is on the target.** Its rules are read from the *source*, so on a first sync to a target lacking the file, the excluded target file is *deleted* — exactly like adding a `.gitignore` line doesn't untrack existing files. The **central `filter_file` has no such lag** — it protects immediately, every run. This is precisely why #166 chose global-first (central filter for enforced protection). I fixed the test to model the realistic steady-state (per-dir files present on both ends) and **documented the asymmetry** in `docs/configuration.md`, recommending the central filter for must-never-delete rules.
2. rsync's default quick-check skipped my overwrite case because source/target were same-size/same-second; fixed by making them differ in size.

After the fixes, the local reproduction reports **all 17 expected target outcomes match real rsync**.

## Verification status

Local gates green: ruff, basedpyright (0/0/0), codespell, no-hardwrap, and the test collects cleanly (7 tests). The **behavioral** proof on real VMs will run whenever you take the PR out of draft — I left it draft per your VM-provisioning work and did not trigger the integration suite.

One thing worth your call: the pure "excluded, nothing on source → survives" case is proven via the **central** filter (which genuinely protects). For **per-dir** files I test the steady-state (filter present on both ends); the first-sync per-dir deletion behavior is now documented rather than asserted. If you'd like, I can add an explicit assertion of that first-sync deletion too — but it's arguably testing rsync's own semantics rather than pc-switcher's.

Finding 1: isn't this a risk? I add the .pcswitcher-filter to a folder to protect the target, but the target gets overwritten/deleted anyway.
Is there something to do about this other than document clearly that these cases should be defined in the central filter_file?

Good question — this is a real design concern, not just a documentation nit. Let me not hand-wave it: I'll (1) verify whether there's a clean technical fix, and (2) check what safety nets the sync flow *already* has, before recommending.

First, the most promising fix: seed the `.pcswitcher-filter` files onto the target in a preliminary non-deleting pass, so per-dir protection is in effect during the main `--delete` pass. Let me verify that actually works against real rsync:

input.command:
cd /tmp/claude-1000/-home-janfr-dev-pc-166/c4320c6a-5df9-4349-ba0d-bb77817e26a7/scratchpad
echo "=== Two-pass: pre-seed .pcswitcher-filter, THEN mirror with --delete ==="
rm -rf tp && mkdir -p tp/src/nest/deep tp/dst/nest/deep
# Source: per-dir filters, keep files, NO secret files on source
printf -- '- secret\n' > tp/src/nest/.pcswitcher-filter
printf -- '- deep_only\n' > tp/src/nest/deep/.pcswitcher-filter
printf 'keep-src' > tp/src/nest/keep.txt
printf 'deep-keep-src' > tp/src/nest/deep/keep.txt
# Target pre-existing (NO .pcswitcher-filter yet): files the per-dir rules would exclude
printf 'tgt-secret' > tp/dst/nest/secret
printf 'tgt-deep-secret' > tp/dst/nest/deep/secret        # protected via inheritance
printf 'tgt-deep-only' > tp/dst/nest/deep/deep_only
printf 'tgt-doomed' > tp/dst/nest/unprotected.txt          # NOT excluded → should be deleted

echo "--- BEFORE (dst) ---"; find tp/dst -type f | sed 's#tp/dst/##' | sort

echo "--- PASS 1: seed only .pcswitcher-filter files, NO --delete ---"
rsync -a --include='*/' --include='.pcswitcher-filter' --exclude='*' tp/src/ tp/dst/
echo "--- PASS 2: real mirror with --delete + dir-merge ---"
rsync -a --delete --filter='dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter' tp/src/ tp/dst/

echo "--- AFTER (dst) ---"; find tp/dst -type f | sed 's#tp/dst/##' | sort
echo "--- checks ---"
for f in nest/secret nest/deep/secret nest/deep/deep_only; do
  test -e "tp/dst/$f" && echo "SURVIVED  $f = $(cat tp/dst/$f)" || echo "DELETED   $f"
done
test -e tp/dst/nest/unprotected.txt && echo "BUG: unprotected survived" || echo "OK: unprotected.txt correctly deleted"

content.text: Two-pass works. But before recommending it, let me check whether a simpler one-flag change — `--delete-after` (deletions happen after transfers, by which point the `.pcswitcher-filter` files are on the target) — fixes it without a second pass:

input.command:
cd /tmp/claude-1000/-home-janfr-dev-pc-166/c4320c6a-5df9-4349-ba0d-bb77817e26a7/scratchpad
setup() {
  rm -rf $1 && mkdir -p $1/src/nest/deep $1/dst/nest/deep
  printf -- '- secret\n' > $1/src/nest/.pcswitcher-filter
  printf -- '- deep_only\n' > $1/src/nest/deep/.pcswitcher-filter
  printf 'keep-src' > $1/src/nest/keep.txt
  printf 'tgt-secret' > $1/dst/nest/secret
  printf 'tgt-deep-secret' > $1/dst/nest/deep/secret
  printf 'tgt-deep-only' > $1/dst/nest/deep/deep_only
  printf 'tgt-doomed' > $1/dst/nest/unprotected.txt
}
check() {
  for f in nest/secret nest/deep/secret nest/deep/deep_only; do
    test -e "$1/dst/$f" && echo "  SURVIVED $f" || echo "  DELETED  $f"
  done
  test -e $1/dst/nest/unprotected.txt && echo "  unprotected SURVIVED (should delete)" || echo "  unprotected deleted (correct)"
}
for flag in "--delete" "--delete-after" "--delete-delay"; do
  setup t_$RANDOM 2>/dev/null; d=$(ls -d t_* | tail -1)
  echo "=== single pass with $flag (no pre-seed) ==="
  rsync -a $flag --filter='dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter' $d/src/ $d/dst/ 2>&1 | grep -i error
  check $d
done

content.text: That's a critical result — and it's worse than "not protected": with `--delete`, the target's file was **deleted entirely** (not left old, not overwritten — gone), because the source excludes it → it's absent from the sender's file list → `--delete` removes it. So update collapses into deletion. You're right that this spans deletes, updates, and adds. Let me establish complete ground truth with a matrix rather than reason piecemeal:

input.command:
cd /tmp/claude-1000/-home-janfr-dev-pc-166/c4320c6a-5df9-4349-ba0d-bb77817e26a7/scratchpad
# Rule lives in the SOURCE tree (normal case: user adds .pcswitcher-filter on their machine, syncs out).
# For each operation, compare single-pass --delete vs pre-seed(filter files)+--delete two-pass.
matrix() {
  op="$1"     # delete | update | add
  mode="$2"   # single | preseed
  rm -rf m && mkdir -p m/src/nest m/dst/nest
  printf -- '- F\n' > m/src/nest/.pcswitcher-filter
  printf 'keep-src' > m/src/nest/keep.txt
  case "$op" in
    delete) printf 'TGT'    > m/dst/nest/F ;;                                  # target-only file, source lacks F
    update) printf 'SRCnew' > m/src/nest/F; printf 'TGTold' > m/dst/nest/F ;;  # both have F (differ), source's excluded
    add)    printf 'SRCnew' > m/src/nest/F ;;                                   # source-only F (excluded), target lacks it
  esac
  if [ "$mode" = preseed ]; then
    rsync -a --include='*/' --include='.pcswitcher-filter' --exclude='*' m/src/ m/dst/
  fi
  rsync -a --delete --filter='dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter' m/src/ m/dst/
  printf "  %-7s %-8s -> target F: %s\n" "$op" "$mode" "$(cat m/dst/nest/F 2>/dev/null || echo ABSENT)"
}
echo "Intent of the per-dir rule '- F': keep the target's F untouched / don't push F."
echo "--- single pass (today's behavior) ---"
for op in delete update add; do matrix $op single; done
echo "--- pre-seed .pcswitcher-filter first, then --delete ---"
for op in delete update add; do matrix $op preseed; done
input.description: Full matrix: delete/update/add x single vs preseed

result.output:
Intent of the per-dir rule '- F': keep the target's F untouched / don't push F.
--- single pass (today's behavior) ---
  delete  single   -> target F: ABSENT
  update  single   -> target F: ABSENT
  add     single   -> target F: ABSENT
--- pre-seed .pcswitcher-filter first, then --delete ---
  delete  preseed  -> target F: TGT
  update  preseed  -> target F: TGTold
  add     preseed  -> target F: ABSENT
